title: podman does not read ~/.config/containers/registries.conf on Windows 10
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

When running podman on Windows 10, the registries.conf file does not seem to be read.

Note that the documentation contains no information where the global registries.conf file should be located, so only the user-local version in ~/.config/containers/registries.conf can be applied. Creating and editing that file has no effect on podman behavior.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Try pulling an image with unqualified repo name. Note that it fails as expected with error: "short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY"

2. Create ~/.config/containers/registries.conf and add the following information: 
```
short-name-mode="permissive"
unqualified-search-registries = ['docker.io']
```
4. Try pulling the image again. Same failure happens.

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without TTY

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successfully pulling image from docker.io without qualifying the name

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
This happens even if CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF is set to a valid location of registries.conf.
I would say there are three issues here:
1: The location of the global registries.conf is undocumented for Windows.
2: The user-local version of registries.conf is not read on Windows.
3: The CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF version of registries.conf is not read on Windows.

**Output of `podman version`:**
```

Version:      4.3.0
API Version:  4.3.0

Git Commit:   ad42af94903ce4f3c3cd0693e4e17e4286bf094b

```

comments.body: This file is read where the podman service runs, so in the podman machine (assuming you use podman machine).
@Luap99 Oooh. That makes sense. I suppose it could have been a bit clearer in the docs, but now I get it. Thank you.
@TheRealHnefi We can always improve the docs. If you have suggestions please let us know or even better open a PR.
